title: Self-Registration Portal Storm Detection and Bot-Onboarding Defense

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 8.5.1 covers MFA / strong auth controls.
compliance.controlObjective: Strong auth control on guest portal.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(b) — incident handling. Bot-onboarding storms qualify as incidents.
AC-2(11) (Usage Conditions) — abnormal-onboarding detection evidences usage-condition monitoring.
A.5.16 (Identity management) — guest identity creation audit.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Script 110 self-registrations from 2 source IPs. UC-17.1.52 fires with `suspicious=likely_bot` for the `gmail.com` domain.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Run 10 organic self-registrations from various IPs. UC-17.1.52 returns `suspicious=normal`.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Self-Registration Portal* category — codes `86100` (self-registration started), `86101` (self-registration completed), `86102` (self-registration failed), `86103` (self-registration captcha failed), `86104` (self-registration rate-limit), `86110` (email validation failure), `86120` (mass self-registration anomaly). Fields: `guest_user`, `email_domain`, `client_ip`, `mac_address`, `failed_reason`.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (86100, 86101, 86102, 86104, 86110)
| stats count, dc(guest_user) as unique_users, dc(client_ip) as unique_ips, dc(mac_address) as unique_macs by email_domain
| eval suspicious = case(
    unique_users > 100 AND unique_ips < 5, "likely_bot",
    unique_users > 50 AND unique_macs/unique_users < 0.5, "likely_bot",
    count > 500, "possible_storm",
    true(), "normal")
| where suspicious != "normal"
description: Detects self-registration portal storms and bot-driven onboarding patterns — characterised by many user creations from few source IPs or MACs.
value: Self-registration portals are a common attack target. Botnets attempt to create thousands of accounts to exhaust IP pools, evade rate-limiting, or onboard malicious devices. Detecting the storm in real-time enables CAPTCHA hardening, IP allow-listing, or temporary portal disable.
implementation: Confirm *Self-Registration Portal* category at INFO. Schedule every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `email_domain` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single value: storm signal (red if `suspicious != normal`). (2) Map: client_ip geographic distribution. (3) Top-N: domains.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **Large event** — A legitimate conference may produce 500+ self-registrations. Cross-reference event calendar. (b) **Captive portal landing-page test** — QA testing of the captive portal can produce burst traffic; tag and exclude.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE Self-Registration portal enabled.
• `event_calendar.csv` for context.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Self-Registration Portal* category at INFO.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Self-Reg Storm":
• Storm signal tile.
• Geographic distribution.
• Top-N domains.

Alerting: PagerDuty high on `likely_bot`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **CAPTCHA still bypassed** — Tune CAPTCHA settings on the portal.
• **Storm persists after IP block** — Attack uses VPN; block by user-agent or behavioural pattern.
grandmaExplanation: A guest sign-up page on the network — like a sign-in sheet at a hotel — can be abused by automated programs creating fake guest accounts. We watch for many sign-ups from few computers and ring the bell when the pattern looks like a bot.
